info.name: Invoice4u API collection
info.description:
## Invoice4u REST API

**Authentication is via your Invoice4u API key only.** Login/session tokens are no longer accepted by the API. The `Invoice4u_ApiKey` collection variable is sent as `token` on every endpoint, and as `Invoice4UUserApiKey` on clearing (`ProcessApiRequestV2`) calls.

Curated collection of `ApiService.svc` examples for integrating against Invoice4u: creating documents, managing customers, charging cards, and looking up clearing logs. Each request explains the business scenario it demonstrates so you can copy it as-is or adapt it.

### Folder layout
- **Authentication** - verify that your API key is active before running any business calls
- **Customers** - create, update, retrieve and search customers
- **Documents** - issue every supported document type (invoice, receipt, invoice-receipt, credit, proforma, order, quote, delivery note), look them up, and perform post-creation actions
- **Branches** - list branches for multi-location organisations
- **Clearing & Payments** - credit-card / Bit charging via `ProcessApiRequestV2`, plus clearing-log queries
- **User Registration** - template only (restricted partner endpoint)

### Variables to set before running
Open the collection's **Variables** tab and fill in:

| Variable | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `baseURL` | API host, e.g. `https://api.invoice4u.co.il` |
| `Invoice4u_ApiKey` | Your Invoice4u API key. Sent as `token` on every endpoint, and as `Invoice4UUserApiKey` on clearing calls. |
| `ClientID` | ID of an existing customer used by the document examples |

### Dynamic helpers (already wired up)
- `{{$guid}}` - Postman built-in, used for `ApiIdentifier` to keep document creation idempotent.
- `{{$timestamp}}` - Unix seconds, useful for ad-hoc identifiers.
- `{{CurrentMsDate}}` - populated by the pre-request script in the Microsoft `/Date(ms+0200)/` format expected by the API.

### Suggested order for first-time integrators
1. Run **Authentication > Verify API Key** to confirm `Invoice4u_ApiKey` is valid.
2. Run **Customers > Create Customer** and copy the returned `ID` into `ClientID`.
3. Run a document example (start with **Documents > Invoice > Existing Customer - minimal taxable invoice**).
4. Optionally run **Documents > Search & Retrieve > Get Document by API Identifier (REST)** with the same `ApiIdentifier` to fetch the document back.

item.name: 🔐 Authentication
item.description: Endpoints used to verify that your API key is active and authorized before running any business calls. Run this once whenever you change `Invoice4u_ApiKey`.

request.description: Returns the `User` object that owns the supplied API key, or an error if the key is invalid / expired. Run this first to confirm `Invoice4u_ApiKey` is set correctly before sending business calls.

item.name: 👥 Customers
item.description: Create, update, search and retrieve customers (`Customer` entity).

body.raw:
{
    "cu": {
        "Name": "Acme Ltd.",
        "Email": "contact@acme.test",
        "Guid": "{{$guid}}",
        "Phone": "03-5555444",
        "Cell": "050-1234567",
        "UniqueID": "514526996",
        "Address": "1 Rothschild Blvd",
        "City": "Tel Aviv",
        "Zip": "6688101",
        "Active": true,
        "IsNonUniqueNameCreation": true
    },
    "token": "{{Invoice4u_ApiKey}}"
}

request.description: Creates a new customer. `Guid` is the external id you control – using `{{$guid}}` ensures every run produces a unique customer. Set `IsNonUniqueNameCreation:true` to allow duplicate names in the same organisation.
Updates an existing customer. `ID` is required and must belong to the authenticated organisation.
Returns the full `Customer` record (including extended fields such as bank details and associated emails). `orgID:0` falls back to the organisation that owns the supplied API key.
Looks up a customer by the external `Guid` you supplied at creation time.
Free-text search across customers. All `cust` fields are optional – any supplied field acts as a `LIKE` filter.

item.name: List All Customers in My Organisation

request.description: Returns every customer belonging to the organisation that owns `Invoice4u_ApiKey`.

item.name: Quick Lookup - Search Customers by Name only

request.description: Shortcut variant of `GetCustomers` that searches by `Name` only. Use this when integrating a 'customer auto-complete' style picker on your side - send the user's typed text as `Name` and display the matches.

item.name: 📄 Documents
item.description:
Document generation, lookup, drafts and post-creation actions.

### Document type cheat-sheet
| Type | Name | Used for |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Invoice | Tax invoice (חשבונית מס) |
| 2 | Receipt | Receipt only (קבלה) – needs `Payments` |
| 3 | InvoiceReceipt | Invoice + receipt combined – needs `Items` **and** `Payments` |
| 4 | InvoiceCredit | Credit / refund invoice (חשבונית זיכוי) – usually references an existing invoice |
| 5 | ProformaInvoice | Pro-forma / חשבון עסקה |
| 6 | InvoiceOrder | Order |
| 7 | InvoiceQuote | Price quote |
| 8 | InvoiceShip | Delivery note (תעודת משלוח) |

### Common fields
- `ClientID` – existing customer; **or** supply `GeneralCustomer` for a one-time client.
- `ApiIdentifier` – your own idempotency key; use `{{$guid}}` to keep it unique.
- `TaxIncluded` – `true` if `Price` already includes VAT.
- `Items[].TaxPercentage` – `0` to mark item as tax-exempt.
- `Payments[].PaymentType` – `1`=CreditCard, `2`=Check, `3`=Transfer, `4`=Cash, `5`=Credit, `6`=Withholding, `7`=Other, `8`=Bit, `9`=PayBox.
- `DocumentReffType` – `1` = receipt for an existing invoice, `2` = cancellation.

item.name: 🧾 Invoice (Type 1)
item.description:
## Tax Invoice (חשבונית מס) – `DocumentType = 1`

Standard tax invoice. Records what the customer owes but **does not** record payment – for a paid document use Invoice-Receipt (Type 3), and for the payment side alone use Receipt (Type 2).

### When to use it
- Standard sale where the customer will pay later (open receivable).
- Converting an approved Quote (Type 7), Order (Type 6), Proforma (Type 5) or Delivery Note (Type 8) into an actual accounting document.

### Required fields
- `ClientID` (or `GeneralCustomer` for walk-ins).
- `DocumentType = 1`.
- `Items[]` – at least one line with `Name`, `Quantity`, `Price`.
- `Currency` (e.g. `"ILS"`) and `TaxIncluded` (`true` if `Price` is VAT-inclusive).

### Optional but recommended
- `ApiIdentifier` (a GUID you generate) – makes the call idempotent, so retries return the same invoice instead of duplicating it.
- `Discount` – flat (`IsNominal:true`) or percentage; `BeforeTax` controls whether it applies before or after VAT.
- `DocumentReffType` + `Invoices[]` – set when converting from a Quote/Order/Proforma/Delivery Note so the source's status is updated.
- `AssociatedEmails[]` – recipients that receive the PDF on creation.

### Common pitfalls
- Do **not** send `Payments[]` here – add them via a Receipt (Type 2) referencing this invoice, or use an Invoice-Receipt (Type 3).
- Foreign-currency invoices need `TaxPercentage = 0` and the destination currency code.
- Once created, an invoice cannot be edited – to fix it, issue a Credit Invoice (Type 4) and reissue.

### After the call
The response contains the new document's `ID` (GUID) and `Number`. Fetch it back via **🔍 Search & Retrieve › Get Document by GUID** or **Get Document by API Identifier**.

request.description: Minimal invoice with a single taxable item. Using `CreateDocumentWithIdentifierValidation` blocks accidental duplicates that share the same `ApiIdentifier` within ~60s.

item.name: Existing Customer - multiple line items (taxable + tax-exempt)

request.description: Demonstrates mixing taxable and tax-exempt items in the same invoice.

item.name: Existing Customer - with flat-amount pre-tax discount

request.description: `Discount.IsNominal=true` ⇒ Value is a flat amount. Set `IsNominal:false` and use a value like `10` to apply a 10% discount instead. `BeforeTax:true` applies the discount on the pre-VAT amount.

Use `GeneralCustomer` (instead of `ClientID`) when you don't want to persist a customer record. `Identifier` is the customer's Israeli ID / company number and is required for tax-invoice compliance over a certain amount.

item.name: Existing Customer - foreign-currency export invoice (USD, 0% VAT)

request.description: Foreign-currency export invoice. `ConversionRate` is the rate vs ILS used by the books; `TaxPercentage:0` is typical for true exports.
Percentage discount variant. `Discount.IsNominal:false` makes `Value` a percent (here, 10%), as opposed to a flat amount. `BeforeTax:true` applies the discount on the pre-VAT amount.

item.name: 💰 Receipt (Type 2)
item.description:
## Receipt (קבלה) – `DocumentType = 2`

A receipt records **payment received**. It carries `Payments[]` but never `Items[]` – the goods/services must already be described on a source Invoice.

### When to use it
- Settling one or more previously issued Tax Invoices (Type 1) or Proformas (Type 5).
- Standalone payment capture (e.g. donation receipts for NGOs).
- Cancelling a previous receipt (`DocumentReffType = 2` + `CancelDocument = true`).

### Required fields
- `ClientID` (or `GeneralCustomer`).
- `DocumentType = 2`.
- `Payments[]` – at least one payment with `Amount`, `PaymentType`, and payment-type-specific fields (e.g. cheque number, credit-card last-4).
- `Currency` matching the source invoice(s).

### Settling existing invoices
- Set `DocumentReffType = 1` (Invoice) or `5` (Proforma).
- List each source under `Invoices[]` with `ID` (GUID), `DocumentType`, `ClientID`, `Total`, and `ReceiptAmount` (how much of it is being paid now).
- Sum of `Invoices[].ReceiptAmount` **must equal** sum of `Payments[].Amount` (`Deduction` counts toward payments). Otherwise the API returns `PaymentAmountDoesntMatchReffDocumentsAmount` (error 55).
- The source must be status `Open` (1) or `PartiallyCredited` (4). Fully-paid invoices are rejected with `DocumentStatusInValid` (error 49).

### Common pitfalls
- Do **not** send `Items[]` – receipts are payment-only.
- `PaymentType` must be one of the values in `PaymentTypes` (1 = CreditCard, 2 = Check, 3 = MoneyTransfer, 4 = Cash, 6 = WithholdingTax, 8 = Bit, 9 = PayBox). Wrong values return `PaymentTypeOutOfRange` (error 51).
- Withholding-tax amounts go in `Deduction`, not in `Payments[]`.

### After the call
The source invoice's status becomes `Closed` (2) once fully paid. Verify via **🔍 Search & Retrieve › Get Document by GUID**.

request.description: Cash receipt for an existing customer. `{{CurrentMsDate}}` is generated by the collection pre-request script in the `/Date(ms+0200)/` format expected by the API.

item.name: Walk-in (one-time) Customer - split cash + credit card

request.description: Same as above, but for a walk-in / non-persistent customer.
`IsDonation:true` flags the receipt as a tax-deductible donation receipt (קבלה לתרומה). Only usable by organisations approved as Section 46 (סעיף 46) institutions.
Closes one or more existing invoices. Set each entry under `Invoices` with the invoice `ID` (GUID), its `DocumentType` (`1` = Invoice) and the amount being settled (`ReceiptAmount`).
Issues a negative receipt that cancels an existing one. Amounts and `Total` must be negative; reference the original receipt under `Invoices`.

item.name: Split payment - cash + credit card + Bit

request.description: Receipt split between cash (4), credit card (1) and Bit (8).

item.name: Receipt with withholding tax (Deduction at source)

request.description: `Deduction` is the amount the customer withheld at source (ניכוי במקור). The receipt totals to `Payments.Amount + Deduction`.

item.name: 🧾💰 Invoice Receipt (Type 3)
item.description:
## Invoice-Receipt (חשבונית מס/קבלה) – `DocumentType = 3`

A combined invoice + receipt in one document. Use this when the customer pays at the moment of sale, so you don't need to issue a separate invoice and receipt.

### When to use it
- Point-of-sale: customer buys and pays in one action.
- Online checkout after successful clearing (`ProcessApiRequestV2` succeeded and you want a single accounting document).
- Closing a Proforma/Order/Quote/Delivery Note where payment is captured at the same time.

### Required fields
- `ClientID` (or `GeneralCustomer`).
- `DocumentType = 3`.
- `Items[]` – the goods/services being sold.
- `Payments[]` – how the customer paid.
- `Currency` and `TaxIncluded`.

### The one hard rule
Sum of `Payments[].Amount` (+ `Deduction`) **must equal** the items total after discount and VAT. Mismatches return `PaymentAmountDoesntMatchItemsAmount` (error 56). If you set `AutoFixPaymentsMismatchItems = true` the server will insert a small correction line for ±0.01 rounding drift; larger drifts still fail.

### Optional
- `Discount`, `AssociatedEmails[]`, `ApiIdentifier` – same semantics as a plain Invoice.
- `DocumentReffType` + `Invoices[]` – set when converting from Proforma (5), Order (6), Quote (7) or Delivery Note (8).

### Common pitfalls
- Do not use Type 3 to settle a previously issued Type 1 invoice – issue a Receipt (Type 2) against it instead.
- For split payments, each entry in `Payments[]` needs its own `PaymentType` and type-specific fields.

### After the call
Single document created with both invoice and receipt numbers. It is immediately status `Closed` (2).

request.description: Standard invoice-receipt for an existing customer.

item.name: Existing Customer - combined doc with flat pre-tax discount

request.description: Same as above but applies a 10₪ flat pre-tax discount. The payment total reflects the post-discount value.
Combined invoice-receipt for a walk-in (one-time) customer.

item.name: Existing Customer - split payment (cash + credit card + Bit)

request.description: Single invoice-receipt whose 100 ILS total is split across cash (PaymentType=4), credit card (1) and Bit (8). The sum of `Payments[].Amount` must equal the items total.

item.name: ↩️ Credit Invoice (Type 4)
item.description:
## Credit Invoice (חשבונית זיכוי) – `DocumentType = 4`

A credit invoice **reverses or reduces** a previously issued Invoice (Type 1) or Invoice-Receipt (Type 3). It is a legally distinct accounting document – you never edit or delete the original; instead you issue a credit that offsets it.

### When to use it
- **Full refund** – the customer is being refunded the entire amount of an invoice.
- **Partial refund** – only part of the original invoice is being reversed (specific line items, wrong quantity, wrong price).
- **Correction** – the original invoice was issued with wrong details and needs to be voided so a corrected invoice can be issued.
- **Bad debt / write-off** – you no longer expect to collect the invoice.

### How to build the request
1. **Pick the source document.** You need its `Guid` (the value returned as `ID` when it was created, or fetched via `GetDocumentByApiIdentifier` / `GetDocumentByGuid`).
2. **Set `DocumentType = 4`** on the outer document.
3. **Set `DocumentReffType` to match the source type** – this is the #1 source of `DocumentReffTypeNotInRange` (error 53) errors:
   - Source is an **Invoice (Type 1)** ⇒ `DocumentReffType = 1`
   - Source is an **Invoice-Receipt (Type 3)** ⇒ `DocumentReffType = 3`
   - Any other value will be rejected.
4. **List the source document(s) under `Invoices[]`.** Each entry needs `ID` (GUID), `DocumentType` (matching `DocumentReffType`), `ClientID`, `Total` (of the source), and `ReceiptAmount` (how much of it you are crediting now).
5. **Set `Total` and `CreditAmount`** on the outer document to the amount actually being credited. They must be equal, and their sum across all past credits cannot exceed the source `Total` (else `CreditAmountExceedsTotal`, error 155).
6. **Add `Items[]` only for a partial credit.** For a full credit against a single invoice you can omit `Items` and the server will credit the full source amount. For a partial credit, list the exact refunded lines so the PDF shows what is being reversed.

### Rules the API will enforce
- The source document must belong to the same organisation and the same `ClientID` (or use a `GeneralCustomer`).
- The source document status must **not** already be `FullyCredited` (3). Partially-credited sources are fine as long as `CreditAmount` doesn't exceed the remaining balance.
- `Invoices[]` is **required** – a credit invoice with no `Invoices`, no `Payments` and no `Items` returns `InvoiceCreditMustHaveRefDocuments` (error 57).
- `ApiIdentifier` (a GUID you generate) makes the call idempotent – re-sending the same identifier returns the existing credit instead of duplicating it. Highly recommended for retry logic.

### After the call
The source document's status becomes `PartiallyCredited` (4) or `FullyCredited` (3) automatically. You can verify with **🔍 Search & Retrieve › Get Document by GUID**.

item.name: Full credit of a Tax Invoice (Type 1 source)

request.description:
**Scenario:** the customer returned everything on a Tax Invoice (Type 1) and you need to refund the full amount.

**What to change before running:**
- `REPLACE-WITH-INVOICE-GUID` → the `ID` (GUID) of the source invoice, e.g. `"a1b2c3d4-...".
- `Total`, `CreditAmount`, `Invoices[0].Total`, `Invoices[0].ReceiptAmount` – set them all to the same amount you are refunding (the source invoice's `Total`).

**Why `DocumentReffType = 1`?** The source is a Type 1 invoice, so the reference type must also be `1`. Mismatching this returns `DocumentReffTypeNotInRange` (error 53).

**Result:** the source invoice moves to status `FullyCredited` (3).

item.name: Partial credit of a Tax Invoice (refund specific line items)

request.description:
**Scenario:** the original invoice was 100 ILS across multiple lines, and you are refunding only one 50 ILS line item.

**What to change before running:**
- `REPLACE-WITH-INVOICE-GUID` → the source invoice GUID.
- `Items[]` → list the specific refunded lines. These appear on the credit PDF, so keep names/codes recognisable to the customer.
- `Invoices[0].Total` → the source invoice's *full* total (e.g. `100`).
- `Invoices[0].ReceiptAmount`, outer `Total`, outer `CreditAmount` → the *partial* amount being refunded now (e.g. `50`). All three must match.

**Result:** the source invoice moves to status `PartiallyCredited` (4). You can issue additional credits against the same source until its remaining balance reaches zero – at that point it becomes `FullyCredited` (3). Exceeding the remaining balance returns `CreditAmountExceedsTotal` (error 155).

item.name: Full credit of an Invoice-Receipt (Type 3 source)

request.description:
**Scenario:** the source document is an Invoice-Receipt (Type 3, חשבונית מס/קבלה) – meaning the customer already paid. You are issuing a credit to reverse both the invoice and the receipt in one document.

**Key difference from Type 1 credits:** you must set both `DocumentReffType = 3` on the outer document **and** `Invoices[0].DocumentType = 3`. Using `1` (which is a common copy-paste from the tax-invoice example) will fail with `DocumentReffTypeNotInRange` (error 53) or `ReceiptDocumentReffTypeDoesntMatchInvoiceDocumentType` (error 54).

**What to change before running:**
- `REPLACE-WITH-INVOICE-RECEIPT-GUID` → the GUID of the Type 3 source.
- Amounts → set `Total`, `CreditAmount`, `Invoices[0].Total`, `Invoices[0].ReceiptAmount` to the source's `Total`.

**Note on the refund itself:** this API call issues the credit *document*. If the original payment was collected via credit-card clearing and you also need to refund the money, use **💳 Clearing & Payments › ↩️ Refund a previously charged transaction** in addition to this call.

item.name: Credit multiple source Invoices in a single credit document

request.description:
**Scenario:** the customer is being refunded for two Tax Invoices at once – e.g. a 100 ILS invoice and an 80 ILS invoice, refunded together as a single 180 ILS credit.

**What to change before running:**
- Replace both `REPLACE-WITH-*-INVOICE-GUID` values with real source GUIDs.
- Each `Invoices[]` entry must reference the **same** `ClientID` – you cannot credit different customers in one document.
- All sources here are Type 1, so `DocumentReffType = 1` and every `Invoices[].DocumentType = 1`. To credit a mixed batch (some Type 1, some Type 3) issue separate credit documents – the reference type is a single value per document.
- Outer `Total` / `CreditAmount` = sum of all `Invoices[].ReceiptAmount`.

**Result:** each source invoice's status is updated independently based on how much of its total was credited in this call.

item.name: 📋 Proforma Invoice (Type 5)
item.description:
## Proforma Invoice (חשבון עסקה) – `DocumentType = 5`

A proforma is a **non-binding** statement of what will be invoiced. It doesn't book VAT and doesn't create a receivable, but it's a document the customer can pay against.

### When to use it
- Pre-billing before delivery – the customer needs a document to trigger a purchase order or payment on their side.
- Advance-payment collection: issue a proforma, take payment via a Receipt (Type 2) with `DocumentReffType = 5`, and later convert into a Tax Invoice (Type 1).
- Government / municipal customers that only pay against a `חשבון עסקה`.

### Required fields
- `ClientID` (or `GeneralCustomer`).
- `DocumentType = 5`.
- `Items[]`, `Currency`, `TaxIncluded`.

### Optional
- `Discount`, `PaymentDueDate` (used for the customer-side due-date reminder), `AssociatedEmails[]`, `ApiIdentifier`.

### Common pitfalls
- A proforma is **not** a tax invoice – the Israeli tax authority does not recognise it as a taxable event. You must eventually issue a Tax Invoice or Invoice-Receipt.
- Do not send `Payments[]` on the proforma itself – attach payments to a subsequent Receipt (Type 2) referencing this proforma.

### After the call
The proforma is created with status `Open` (1) and can be referenced from a later Receipt or Invoice-Receipt via `DocumentReffType = 5`.

request.description: Simple proforma.

item.name: Existing Customer - proforma with flat pre-tax discount

request.description: 10₪ off, applied pre-VAT.

item.name: 📦 Order (Type 6)
item.description:
## Sales Order (הזמנה) – `DocumentType = 6`

Internal / customer-facing document representing goods or services **committed to be delivered**. Not a taxable event by itself.

### When to use it
- Customer has approved a quote and you want a formal order record before shipping / performing the work.
- Fulfilment workflows where an Order is created first and then converted into a Delivery Note (Type 8) and finally an Invoice (Type 1) or Invoice-Receipt (Type 3).

### Required fields
- `ClientID` (or `GeneralCustomer`).
- `DocumentType = 6`.
- `Items[]`, `Currency`, `TaxIncluded`.

### Converting from a Quote
- An Order can be created from a Quote (Type 7) by setting `DocumentReffType = 7` and listing the source under `Invoices[]`. This is the **only** valid reference for an Order – other reference types return `DocumentReffTypeNotInRange` (error 53).

### Common pitfalls
- Orders are not accounting documents. Reports (VAT, income) won't include them until they are converted to a Tax Invoice or Invoice-Receipt.
- Don't attach `Payments[]` – handle payment on the resulting Invoice/Receipt.

### After the call
Order created with status `Open` (1). Reference it from a later Delivery Note, Invoice, or Invoice-Receipt to keep the fulfilment chain linked.

request.description: Single-line order for an existing customer.

item.name: Existing Customer - order with flat pre-tax discount

request.description: Order with a flat 10₪ pre-tax discount.

item.name: 💬 Quote / Price Offer (Type 7)
item.description:
## Price Quote (הצעת מחיר) – `DocumentType = 7`

A proposal you send to a prospect. Nothing is booked accounting-wise; the document exists to be approved (or declined).

### When to use it
- Pre-sale proposal that the customer needs to see before committing.
- Feeding a downstream Order (Type 6), Invoice (Type 1), or Invoice-Receipt (Type 3) once approved.

### Required fields
- `ClientID` (or `GeneralCustomer`).
- `DocumentType = 7`.
- `Items[]`, `Currency`, `TaxIncluded`.

### Optional
- `PaymentDueDate` – shown to the customer as the quote's validity date.
- `Discount`, `AssociatedEmails[]`, `ApiIdentifier`.

### Converting a Quote
A Quote is a valid reference for **Invoice (1)**, **Invoice-Receipt (3)** and **Order (6)** creations – set `DocumentReffType = 7` on the target and list the quote GUID under `Invoices[]`. The quote's status will update accordingly.

### Common pitfalls
- No `Payments[]`, no `Deduction` – a quote is purely informational.
- Editing a quote is not supported – to correct it, use **📧 Distribution & Actions › Change Document Status** to cancel and issue a new one.

### After the call
Quote created with status `Open` (1). Send it to the customer via **📧 Distribution & Actions › Send Document by Email**.

request.description: Simple quote.

item.name: Existing Customer - quote with flat pre-tax discount

request.description: Quote with 10₪ flat pre-tax discount.

item.name: 🚚 Delivery Note (Type 8)
item.description:
## Delivery Note (תעודת משלוח) – `DocumentType = 8`

Accompanies a physical shipment of goods. Not a taxable event – it records **what left the warehouse and when**.

### When to use it
- Shipping physical goods that will be invoiced later.
- Fulfilling an approved Order (Type 6) – the resulting Invoice or Invoice-Receipt can then reference the delivery note.

### Required fields
- `ClientID` (or `GeneralCustomer`) – must include a shipping address on the customer record.
- `DocumentType = 8`.
- `Items[]` with quantities that reflect what is physically shipped.
- `Currency`, `TaxIncluded` (kept for consistency even though no VAT is booked).

### Shipping-specific fields
- `DepartureHour` (0–23) and `DepartureMinute` (0–59) – time the shipment leaves.
- The source Order can be referenced by setting `DocumentReffType = 6` and listing it under `Invoices[]` (the only valid reference type for a Delivery Note).

### Common pitfalls
- Delivery notes don't book revenue. Always issue an Invoice (Type 1) or Invoice-Receipt (Type 3) afterwards for accounting.
- Items on the delivery note should match the physical shipment quantity, which may be less than the ordered quantity for partial fulfilment.

### After the call
Delivery note created with status `Open` (1). Reference it from a later Invoice / Invoice-Receipt so the fulfilment chain (Order → Delivery Note → Invoice) stays linked.

item.name: Existing Customer - delivery note with departure time

request.description: Delivery note with a departure time of 21:29.

item.name: Existing Customer - delivery note with flat pre-tax discount

request.description: Delivery note with a 10₪ pre-tax discount.

item.name: 🔍 Search & Retrieve
item.description:
## Search & Retrieve

Read-only endpoints for finding documents after they've been created. Use these to reconcile your system with Invoice4u, fetch PDFs for archival, or verify idempotency.

### Which lookup to use
- **By `ApiIdentifier`** – the recommended integration pattern. If you saved a GUID as `ApiIdentifier` at creation time, this call returns the exact document (or an empty result if it never made it in). Enables safe retries.
- **By GUID (`ID`)** – when you have the internal document GUID (returned in the creation response).
- **By `Number`** – when the customer or accountant references a printed document number.
- **By date range** – bulk pulls for reporting / reconciliation. Pageable, filterable by `DocumentType`.
- **Get Document PDF** – returns the rendered PDF as a `byte[]` (base64 over JSON) for archival or re-sending.

### Common pitfalls
- Document GUIDs are **not** the same as customer GUIDs. Use `GetCustomerByGuid` for customers.
- Date-range searches are inclusive on both ends; timezone is the organisation's local time (Asia/Jerusalem for Israeli accounts).
- The PDF endpoint returns the current render – if you changed the document's status (e.g. cancelled it) the PDF reflects that.

request.description: Searches documents of a given `DocumentType` within a date / amount range. Set fields to `null`/`0` to ignore them.
Returns the full document by its primary key (GUID).
Resolves a document by the printed running number + its type.
Idempotency lookup: returns the document originally created with the supplied `ApiIdentifier` (e.g. `{{$guid}}`).
Renders the document PDF and returns it as a byte array. Note this hits the **PrintService**, not `ApiService`.

item.name: 📧 Distribution & Actions
item.description:
## Distribution & Actions

Operations you run on documents **after** they've been created – sending them to the customer and changing their lifecycle state.

### Send Document by Email
Delivers the PDF to one or more recipients on the fly. Useful when the recipient list wasn't known at creation time or when re-sending after a bounce.
- The document must already exist; supply its GUID.
- Multiple `AssociatedEmails[]` addresses are supported.
- The from-address and template are controlled by the organisation's settings, not per call.

### Change Document Status
Moves a document between valid states in `EDocumentStatus`:
- `Open` (1), `Closed` (2), `FullyCredited` (3), `PartiallyCredited` (4), `Cancelled` (5).
- Most transitions happen automatically (e.g. paying an invoice closes it, issuing a credit updates the source). Use this endpoint for the exceptions – typically cancelling a Quote / Order / Delivery Note that will never be fulfilled.
- Cancelling an already-issued **Tax Invoice** or **Invoice-Receipt** is not allowed via status change – issue a Credit Invoice (Type 4) instead.

### Common pitfalls
- Changing status doesn't retroactively remove accounting entries – for a booked document you must credit it.
- Email sending is asynchronous; a `200 OK` means the request was accepted, not that the recipient has received it. Check clearing / send logs for delivery status.

request.description: Re-sends an existing document to one or more recipients.
Updates the workflow status of one or more documents. `StatusID` values correspond to the `EDocumentStatus` enum on the server.

item.name: 🏢 Branches
item.description: Branch lookup. Branches are used for multi-location organisations.

request.description: Returns all branches accessible to the authenticated user.

item.name: 💳 Clearing & Payments
item.description:
Credit-card and wallet clearing via `ProcessApiRequestV2`. All scenarios share the same envelope (`request: { ... }`) – the toggles `AddToken`, `AddTokenAndCharge`, `ChargeWithToken`, `Refund`, `IsStandingOrderClearance`, `IsBitPayment`, `IsGooglePay` and `IsApplePay` switch the behaviour.

### Common fields
- `Invoice4UUserApiKey` – set to `{{Invoice4u_ApiKey}}` (collection variable).
- `Sum` – charge amount.
- `PaymentsNum` – number of instalments (use `1` for a single charge).
- `IsDocCreate:true` – automatically issue an InvoiceReceipt (Type 3) for the charge.
- `CallBackUrl` / `ReturnUrl` – where the client is redirected once the hosted page completes.
- `IsGeneralClient:true` + `IsAutoCreateCustomer:true` – auto-create a one-time customer from the client name.

### Wallet payments (Bit / Google Pay / Apple Pay)
Each wallet is enabled by a single boolean on the request – **exactly one** wallet flag per call:
- `IsBitPayment:true` → hosted page opens the Bit flow.
- `IsGooglePay:true` → hosted page opens the Google Pay sheet.
- `IsApplePay:true` → hosted page opens the Apple Pay sheet (Safari / iOS only).

All three require the wallet to be **enabled on your clearing account**. If it's not, the API returns `ApiBitNotAllowedForUser`, `ApiGooglePayNotAllowedForUser` (error 316) or `ApiApplePayNotAllowedForUser` (error 317). Contact support to enable a wallet before running these examples in production.

### Split-tender payments (combining wallets on one order)
`ProcessApiRequestV2` handles **one** payment method per call. To collect a single order total across multiple wallets (e.g. 60 ILS on card + 40 ILS on Bit), issue **one call per slice** and tie them together by reusing the same `OrderIdClientUsage` GUID – the server stores it as `ClearingTraceId`, so all slices show up under one trace when you query the clearing log. Set `IsDocCreate:true` only on the **last** slice (or on none, and issue a manual Invoice-Receipt afterwards) so you don't create one accounting document per slice.

body.raw:
{
    "request": {
        "Invoice4UUserApiKey": "{{Invoice4u_ApiKey}}",
        "Type": "1",
        "CreditCardCompanyType": "1",
        "FullName": "Client Name",
        "Phone": "0500000000",
        "Email": "client@acme.test",
        "Sum": "1",
        "Description": "Invoice4U Clearing",
        "PaymentsNum": "1",
        "Currency": "ILS",
        "OrderIdClientUsage": "{{$guid}}",
        "IsDocCreate": "true",
        "DocHeadline": "Document headline",
        "Comments": "Document comments",
        "IsManualDocCreationsWithParams": "false",
        "DocItemQuantity": "1|1",
        "DocItemPrice": "10|10",
        "DocItemTaxRate": "17|17",
        "IsItemsBase64Encoded": "false",
        "DocItemName": "first item|second item",
        "IsGeneralClient": "true",
        "IsAutoCreateCustomer": "true",
        "CallBackUrl": "https://webhook.site/123153",
        "ReturnUrl": "https://www.invoice4u.co.il/",
        "AddToken": "false",
        "AddTokenAndCharge": "false",
        "ChargeWithToken": "false",
        "Refund": "false",
        "IsStandingOrderClearance": "false",
        "StandingOrderDuration": "0"
    }
}

request.description: Generates a hosted-payment-page URL for a one-off credit-card charge. Pipe-separated `DocItem*` fields define the line items of the auto-created InvoiceReceipt.

}

request.description: Same as regular clearing but `IsBitPayment:true` routes the hosted page through Bit. Requires Bit to be enabled on your clearing account – otherwise the API returns `ApiBitNotAllowedForUser`.

request.description: Same envelope as regular clearing – `IsGooglePay:true` routes the hosted page through Google Pay. Requires Google Pay to be enabled on your clearing account (otherwise the API returns `ApiGooglePayNotAllowedForUser`, error 316). Do **not** set `IsBitPayment` or `IsApplePay` alongside it – one wallet flag per call.

request.description: Same envelope as regular clearing – `IsApplePay:true` routes the hosted page through Apple Pay. Requires Apple Pay to be enabled on your clearing account (otherwise the API returns `ApiApplePayNotAllowedForUser`, error 317). Apple Pay only renders on Safari / iOS; on other browsers the hosted page will fall back to the standard card form. Do **not** set `IsBitPayment` or `IsGooglePay` alongside it – one wallet flag per call.

item.name: 🔗 Split-tender – Card + Bit (2 slices, shared OrderIdClientUsage)

request.description:
**Scenario:** the customer wants to pay a single 100 ILS order in two parts – 60 ILS on a credit card and 40 ILS on Bit.

**How to run:**
1. Generate one GUID and use it as `OrderIdClientUsage` in **both** slices – replace `SHARED-ORDER-GUID-REPLACE-ME` with that GUID. This ties the slices together in the clearing log via `ClearingTraceId`.
2. Send this request first (card slice, `Sum:60`, `IsDocCreate:false`).
3. Duplicate the request, change `Sum` to `40`, add `"IsBitPayment": true`, and set `IsDocCreate:true` on that last slice so a single Invoice-Receipt is issued for the full 100 ILS.

**Notes:**
- Only one wallet flag per call – for a card slice, leave all `Is*Pay` flags out.
- Each slice returns its own `PaymentId` / clearing log row. Use **🔍 Search Clearing Logs** with `OrderIdClientUsage` to reconcile.
- If any slice fails, refund the successful ones via the ↩️ Refund request (same `OrderIdClientUsage`).

item.name: 🔗 Split-tender – Card + Bit + Google Pay (3 slices)

request.description:
**Scenario:** a single 120 ILS order paid across three wallets – 50 ILS on a card, 40 ILS on Bit, 30 ILS on Google Pay. This request is **slice 1 of 3** (the card slice).

**How to run:**
1. Generate one GUID and use it as `OrderIdClientUsage` on **all three** slices – replace `SHARED-ORDER-GUID-REPLACE-ME`.
2. Send this request as-is (`Sum:50`, no wallet flag = card).
3. Duplicate + edit for slice 2: `Sum:40`, add `"IsBitPayment": true`.
4. Duplicate + edit for slice 3: `Sum:30`, add `"IsGooglePay": true`, and set `"IsDocCreate": "true"` so a single Invoice-Receipt is issued for the full 120 ILS at the end. You could just as easily swap Google Pay for Apple Pay (`"IsApplePay": true`) on the last slice.

**Notes:**
- Never send more than one `Is*Pay` flag on the same call – the API will still accept the request, but the routing rules (Bit > Apple Pay > Google Pay) mean only one wallet actually clears. Split them across separate calls instead.
- Slice totals must sum to your true order total. There's no server-side validation across slices – reconciliation is your responsibility via `OrderIdClientUsage`.
- Each wallet must be enabled on your clearing account, otherwise the failing slice returns its own `ApiBitNotAllowedForUser` / `ApiGooglePayNotAllowedForUser` / `ApiApplePayNotAllowedForUser` error and the other slices are still committed.
Tokenisation only – the customer's card is verified and stored as a token for future use; no money is captured.

body.raw:
{
    "request": {
        "Invoice4UUserApiKey": "{{Invoice4u_ApiKey}}",
        "Type": "1",
        "CreditCardCompanyType": "1",
        "FullName": "Client Name",
        "Phone": "0500000000",
        "Email": "client@acme.test",
        "Sum": "2",
        "Description": "Recurring charge",
        "PaymentsNum": "1",
        "Currency": "ILS",
        "OrderIdClientUsage": "{{$guid}}",
        "IsDocCreate": "true",
        "DocumentHeadline": "Document headline",
        "Comments": "Document comments",
        "DocItemQuantity": "1|1",
        "DocItemPrice": "10|10",
        "DocItemTaxRate": "17|17",
        "DocItemName": "first item|second item",
        "IsGeneralClient": "true",
        "IsAutoCreateCustomer": "true",
        "ReturnUrl": "https://www.invoice4u.co.il/",
        "ChargeWithToken": "true",
        "AddToken": "false",
        "AddTokenAndCharge": "false",
        "Refund": "false"
    }
}

request.description: Charges a previously-tokenised customer **without** showing a hosted page. The token is resolved server-side from the customer details.

body.raw:
{
    "request": {
        "Invoice4UUserApiKey": "{{Invoice4u_ApiKey}}",
        "Type": "1",
        "CreditCardCompanyType": "1",
        "FullName": "Client Name",
        "Phone": "0500000000",
        "Email": "client@acme.test",
        "Sum": "1",
        "Description": "First charge + tokenise",
        "PaymentsNum": "1",
        "Currency": "ILS",
        "OrderIdClientUsage": "{{$guid}}",
        "IsDocCreate": "true",
        "DocumentHeadline": "Document headline",
        "Comments": "Document comments",
        "DocItemQuantity": "1|1",
        "DocItemPrice": "10|10",
        "DocItemTaxRate": "17|17",
        "DocItemName": "first item|second item",
        "IsGeneralClient": "true",
        "IsAutoCreateCustomer": "true",
        "ReturnUrl": "https://www.invoice4u.co.il/",
        "ChargeWithToken": "false",
        "AddToken": "true",
        "AddTokenAndCharge": "true",
        "Refund": "false"
    }
}

request.description: Captures money **and** stores the token in the same hosted-page session.
Opens a 12-month standing order. The first charge fires immediately; subsequent charges are billed on the same day each month and notified to `StandingOrderCallBackUrl`.
`Refund:true` reverses a charge and (when `IsDocCreate:true`) issues a credit invoice.
Fetches a single clearing-log entry by its numeric ID.
Searches clearing logs by combination of `PaymentId`, amount range and success flag. All fields are optional.

item.name: 👤 User Registration
item.description: ⚠️ **Restricted endpoint** – this is only available to a small number of partners (e.g. resellers provisioning sub-accounts). Most integrations should **not** need this. Kept here as a template only.

request.description: Provisions a new Invoice4u organisation. Both `token` and `uniqueToken` are partner-issued and must be supplied by Invoice4u – do **not** use this endpoint without coordinating with the Invoice4u team.

exec: // Populate {{CurrentMsDate}} with the current time formatted as Microsoft JSON Date
// (e.g. "/Date(1700000000000+0200)/") - the format expected by Invoice4u for Date fields.
// Light sanity check shared across the collection.

variable.description: Base URL of the Invoice4u API (e.g. https://api.invoice4u.co.il or http://localhost).
Your Invoice4u API key. Sent as `token` on all business endpoints and as `Invoice4UUserApiKey` on clearing (`ProcessApiRequestV2`) endpoints. Login/session tokens are no longer supported.
ID of an existing customer used by the document examples.
Auto-populated by the collection pre-request script with /Date(ms+0200)/ formatted current time.
